confidence.disclosure: Reconstructed from Haydar Çelebi's Ruznâme — the day-by-day campaign diary of a chancery official who accompanied Selim on the Chaldiran, Dulkadir, and Mamluk campaigns — together with Celalzade Mustafa's Selimnâme and the Mamluk chronicler Ibn Iyas for the Egyptian phase. Coverage is unusually precise: Haydar Çelebi records staging halts, marches, and dates to the day from the army's departure from Edirne in March 1514 through the Cairo campaign of 1517.

sources: Haydar Çelebi, Ruznâme-i Gazavât-ı Sultan Selim Han (c. 1520)
Ibn Iyas, Bada'i' al-Zuhur fi Waqa'i' al-Duhur (c. 1522)
Feridun M. Emecen, Yavuz Sultan Selim (2010)

journey.event: Born at Amasya, where his father Bayezid II was serving as provincial governor.
Appointed governor of the Black Sea province of Trabzon, his base for 25 years.
Crossed the Black Sea to the Ottoman Crimean outpost of Kefe to rally forces against his father.
Compelled his father Bayezid II to abdicate and was enthroned as sultan in Istanbul in April 1512.
Moved against his brothers; defeated and killed Ahmed near Bursa.
Assembled the imperial army at Edirne and departed for the Safavid campaign on 20 March 1514.
Passed through Istanbul in April 1514.
Crossed to Asia at Izmit on the Gulf.
Camped at Bolu on the march east.
Halted at Ankara.
Rested the army at Kayseri in central Anatolia.
Reviewed the army at Sivas and suppressed unrest in the ranks.
Passed through Erzincan on the eastern march.
Reached Erzurum, the last major staging point before the frontier.
Crossed into Safavid territory at Tercan.
Decisively defeated Shah Ismail I at the Battle of Chaldiran on 23 August 1514 using artillery and janissary firepower.
Entered the Safavid capital of Tabriz in September 1514 and stayed about a week, deporting artisans to Istanbul.
Wintered the army at Karabağ before the return west.
Wintered at Amasya and executed his grand vizier Hersekzade Ahmed Pasha.
Defeated and killed the Dulkadir ruler Alauddevle, annexing the Dulkadir principality in Cilicia.
Returned to Edirne and prepared a new army for the Mamluk campaign.
Marched the army south through Konya.
Crossed into the Mamluk border region at Adana.
Took Ayntab (Gaziantep) on the advance toward Syria.
Crushed the Mamluk army of Qansuh al-Ghuri at the Battle of Marj Dabiq on 24 August 1516; the Mamluk sultan died on the field.
Entered Aleppo in triumph and received the Abbasid shadow caliph al-Mutawakkil III.
Passed through Hama on the march south.
Continued to Homs.
Entered Damascus in September 1516 and held court for three months while planning the invasion of Egypt.
Marched south through Safed in Galilee.
Accepted the submission of Jerusalem in late December 1516 or early January 1517.
Took Gaza as the army advanced on Egypt.
Crossed the Sinai desert to reach Egypt.
Defeated the new Mamluk sultan Tumanbay at the Battle of Ridaniya outside Cairo on 22 January 1517.
Entered Cairo after three days of street fighting; stayed eight months reorganising Egypt as an Ottoman province.
Visited Alexandria before the return journey.
Recrossed the Sinai and reached Gaza in September 1517.
Returned to Damascus in October 1517.
Left Aleppo on the long march back to Istanbul.
Returned in triumph to Istanbul in July 1518 after nearly two years in the field.
Died of an infection (reportedly anthrax or skin cancer) at Çorlu in Thrace on 22 September 1520 while preparing a new European campaign.
